All right, so we're going to give you a very brief tutorial of Google Sheets and specifically how to use Google Sheets to keep track of your important data and reports. So this is not a tutorial on how to use Google Sheets. Google provides plenty of examples of how to do this. We've provided links in the manual, but you know, basic navigation, you've got basic stuff up here in all your most important buttons. But again, you'll be able to find the documentation. What we want to focus on here is how we're going to use this thing to actually keep track of your stats. So, generally speaking, you can obviously design this in any way that you want. But what we've found works the best is when you put your actual stat names that you want to track here on the left hand column, okay, of course you could do it at the top. But as you grow and expand, it's going to become more difficult. The first thing that you have to just get very clear on is how often are you going to collect this data. So at first, realistically, it might not be every day, you know, maybe you don't have enough sales to do that or you don't have the manpower to make it happen. You know, in this example, this is just one of Grant's webinars, the 10x income system. If you haven't checked it out yet, definitely go to 10xwebclass.com. But we were collecting stats here on a daily basis. Since then, we've actually automated this, but this is a perfect example to show you. So what you'll see here is all of the names of the stats. So how many sales did we get? And just tracking that every single day. And then we put a column, a new column for every single day. It could be every week. It could be every month. But that's just a very neat, orderly way to do it. And one thing that we'll note here just very quickly is to make sure that you have clarity, especially if you're going to be tracking stats once a week. The way we do it at 10x is when we do weekly stats, the stats always end Sunday at midnight. So the new week begins Monday at midnight and it ends Sunday at midnight. So just keeping track of that is really important so that you're consistent with your tracking. You can see how many new sales we made, our average cart value. And it can get very difficult to keep track of what's what. So what Google did is they give you these little bars that you can actually freeze rows and columns.
